Added directory validation and comprehensive tests for system_background_detection_script. Tests cover: custom script execution (exit 0/1), fallback for non-existent script, non-executable script, and directory paths. All 62 tests pass.

feat(schedule): memoize parse_time results with nil caching fix Implement parse_time caching in schedule.lua: - Add module-level cache table and _CACHE_NIL sentinel for nil results - Use rawget to distinguish

Fixed parse_time memoization in schedule.lua with proper nil caching using sentinel value and O(1) cache size tracking. Addresses all issues from previous review: (1) Fixed Lua ternary pitfall where 'a and nil or c' fails because nil is falsy, (2) Replaced O(n) pairs() counting with _cache_size counter, (3) Added tests that monkey-patch string.match to prove caching actually works. All 91 tests pass (23 schedule tests including 4 new cache tests).

feat(schedule): add cache for next_change_at() results Add module-level cache _next_change_cache to avoid recalculating schedule boundaries repeatedly for the same (parsed, time_mins) pair. - Cache uses vim.inspect(parsed) as hash for parsed schedule comparison - Single-entry cache with replacement strategy (no explicit clear needed) - Returns cached result when both time_mins and parsed_hash match - Recomputes and updates cache on mismatch Task: task-4

Added cache for next_change_at() results in schedule.lua with single-entry cache keyed by (time_mins, parsed_hash). Cache uses vim.inspect(parsed) for hash computation and replacement strategy. Added 4 tests verifying caching behavior with same/different inputs. All 27 schedule tests pass.
